_about: The fleet-operations headline numbers a reader most easily mis-reads as throughput/productivity when they are honesty ratios or resolved-only subsets. Both disclose their true shape at the surface (formula inline; the 'humans drained it, not the dispatcher' fence).

rows.invited_intent: throughput - how many issues the fleet closes per period
rows.literal: an HONESTY ratio = TRUE_RESOLVED / (TRUE_RESOLVED + CLAIMED_CLOSED); a low value means few CLOSED issues are git-witnessed as resolved, not that few were closed

rows.invited_intent: the dispatcher's own productivity - how fast the loop is shipping
rows.literal: completed-per-hour counts resolved (not wontfix/dup) closes from ANY actor; a gh-rate far above the loop-rate means humans/peers drained the backlog, not the dispatcher
rows.disclosure: humans/peers are draining the backlog, not the dispatcher
